items.title: U.S. FDA Approves Lupin's Ranluspec (ranibizumab-hkdz) as Interchangeable Biosimilar to Lucentis
items.summary: Lupin Limited has received U.S. FDA approval for Ranluspec™ (ranibizumab-hkdz), an interchangeable biosimilar to Lucentis® (ranibizumab) for the treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration (AMD), diabetic macular edema (DME), and other serious retinal diseases. It is the first interchangeable biosimilar ranibizumab available in both vials and pre-filled syringes (PFS) in the United States.
items.compliance_impact: This approval expands interchangeable biosimilar options in ophthalmology. Health systems and pharmacy networks can substitute Ranluspec for Lucentis without requiring provider intervention, subject to state laws.
items.key_actions:
1. Update pharmacy formularies to include Ranluspec as a cost-effective alternative to Lucentis.
2. Review state-specific laws regarding interchangeable biosimilar substitution.
3. Educate staff on the availability of both vial and pre-filled syringe (PFS) options.

items.title: ICH M15 Guidance: General Principles for Model-Informed Drug Development
items.summary: The FDA has announced the availability of the final guidance for industry, 'M15 General Principles for Model-Informed Drug Development.' Developed by the International Council for Harmonisation (ICH), this document provides a harmonized framework for planning, evaluating, and documenting Model-Informed Drug Development (MIDD) evidence to ensure consistent and high-quality applications across global regulatory agencies.
items.compliance_impact: This guidance establishes a standardized framework for the modeling, simulation, and documentation of MIDD evidence. Organizations must align early regulatory engagement, model assumptions, and credibility assessment with these new expectations.
items.key_actions:
1. Review modeling protocols against the new ICH M15 guidelines.
2. Assess model credibility and 'fit for purpose' based on the risk-based framework.
3. Establish standard operating procedures (SOPs) for multidisciplinary alignment on model assumptions prior to FDA submission.
